evidence_excerpt: During admission for a saddle PE, CTA showed "increase in size of right lower lobe nodule and a new left upper lobe nodule." Care escalated with anticoagulation and IVC filter placement, and discharge instructions referenced hospice nursing, indicating worsening disease and end-of-life care planning.

quality_of_life.evidence_excerpt: At discharge he remained "Confused - always" and "Lethargic but arousable" with ongoing RLE edema and a palpable right groin hematoma; hospice involvement noted ("Please contact hospice nurse").

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: Anticoagulation initiated for PE: "Received heparin without bolus" and "You were started on blood thinning medication."
IVC filter placement documented under Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure.
Right groin complication after IVC filter: "right groin with palpable hematoma, ecchymosis."
New cardiac arrhythmia: "ECG showed new Afib with RBBB."
Severe thromboembolic event: CTA showed "Saddle embolus with segmental pulmonary emboli involving nearly all segments."
Worsening pulmonary nodules: CTA noted "increase in size of right lower lobe nodule and a new left upper lobe nodule."
Transition toward end-of-life care and decreased function: Discharge referenced hospice nurse and noted dependence for mobility and persistent confusion.

notes_on_uncertainty: No follow-up beyond discharge is available. Death is not documented. Functional status and symptom burden were assessed at discharge; hospice involvement suggests persistent severe impairment, but exact long-term outcomes are not recorded.
